\begin{table}[htbp]
  \centering
  \caption{Paired comparison of adjacently ranked access layers on the deep
    fetch (MySQL), over the 25 repeated runs. Because
    every layer runs the identical per-replicate request stream, layers are compared
    on their per-replicate throughput ratios: median throughput (req/s; the per-cell
    bootstrap CI is in the pattern tables), the geometric-mean paired ratio A$/$B with a within-campaign
    paired bootstrap 95\% CI, paired dominance (fraction of replicates in which A exceeds B), and the
    two-sided paired sign-flip permutation $p$ (20000 permutations; a value
    of $5.0{\times}10^{-5}$ is the resolution floor $1/(B{+}1)=1/20{,}001$, i.e.
    the observed statistic exceeded every permutation; the Wilcoxon signed-rank
    test agrees, replication package).}
  \label{tab:significance}
  \begin{adjustbox}{max width=\textwidth}
  \begin{tabular}{l r r r r r}
    \toprule
    Comparison (A $>$ B) & med.\ A & med.\ B & ratio A$/$B [95\% CI] & dom. & perm.\ $p$ \\
    \midrule
    mysql2 $>$ knex & 2382 & 2007 & 1.19~[1.18--1.20] & 1.00 & $5.0{\times}10^{-5}$ \\
    knex $>$ drizzle & 2007 & 1318 & 1.53~[1.51--1.55] & 1.00 & $5.0{\times}10^{-5}$ \\
    drizzle $>$ typeorm & 1318 & 1017 & 1.30~[1.28--1.32] & 1.00 & $5.0{\times}10^{-5}$ \\
    typeorm $>$ sequelize & 1017 & 886 & 1.15~[1.13--1.16] & 1.00 & $5.0{\times}10^{-5}$ \\
    sequelize $>$ objection & 886 & 834 & 1.05~[1.03--1.07] & 0.88 & 0.0001 \\
    objection $>$ prisma & 834 & 634 & 1.33~[1.31--1.36] & 1.00 & $5.0{\times}10^{-5}$ \\
    prisma $>$ mikroorm & 634 & 480 & 1.31~[1.29--1.33] & 1.00 & $5.0{\times}10^{-5}$ \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}

\end{table}
